title: Jianxiawo is back, yet Jiangxi's lithium balance holds firm
gist: CATL's restart of its Jianxiawo lepidolite mine in Jiangxi will lift near-term output to 62,500 tonnes LCE in 2026, but offsetting suspensions and deferrals elsewhere in the province keep Benchmark's Q2 supply-balance and price forecast essentially unchanged.

insights.claim: CATL secured the safety production permit for its Jianxiawo lepidolite mine in Jiangxi, China on 29 June, clearing the last official hurdle to restart a mine idle since August 2025 when its permit expired amid a province-wide enforcement crackdown.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Confirms a supply source is coming back online sooner than expected; monitor whether the restart pressures spot lithium pricing.
insights.quote: CATL secured the safety production permit for its Jianxiawo lepidolite mine in Jiangxi, China on 29 June, the last official hurdle to restarting a mine that has sat idle since August 2025
insights.locator: CATL secured the safety production permit

insights.claim: The Jianxiawo restart arrives slightly ahead of the September timing Benchmark's Q2 Lithium forecast initially assumed, with 62,500 tonnes LCE now forecast to be mined from the operation in 2026.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Earlier-than-modeled timing raises the 2026 output figure; a marker for revising near-term supply models.
insights.quote: The restart arrives slightly ahead of the September timing Benchmark's Q2 Lithium forecast had initially assumed, with 62,500 tonnes LCE now forecast to be mined from Jianxiawo in 2026.

insights.claim: Offsetting suspensions and deferrals elsewhere in Jiangxi province leave Benchmark's Q2 lithium supply balance and price forecast essentially unchanged despite the Jianxiawo restart.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Net-neutral provincial balance means the restart headline is not a bearish price catalyst on its own; watch the offsetting cuts.
insights.quote: offsetting suspensions and deferrals elsewhere in the province leave Benchmark's Q2 lithium forecast supply balance and price forecast essentially unchanged.

insights.claim: Jianxiawo's forecast 2026 output of 62,500 tonnes LCE equates to roughly 1.5% of global mined lithium supply, with the operation ramping toward its 150,000tpa nameplate capacity by 2029.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Sizes the mine's global relevance and signals a multi-year output ramp that will grow its share of supply through 2029.
insights.quote: This equates to roughly 1.5% of global mined supply, with the operation ramping toward its 150,000tpa nameplate capacity by 2029.
